chain.thought:
Add necessary columns to the table.
The question asks about the number of sites listed in a certain location. We need to know the date listed for each site. We extract the value from column "date listed" and create a different column "listing date" for each row. The datatype is datetype.

Select relevant rows.
The question aims to find out how many sites were listed in the year 1980. We need to look at the "date listed" column in each row and count how many times the year 1980 appears.

Filter out useless columns.
similar words of the question link to columns:
listed -> date listed
column value of the question link to columns:
1980 -> date listed
semantic sentence of the question link to columns:
sites listed in 1980 -> date listed

The question is asking to determine the number of sites that were listed in the year 1980. Since each row represents a site with its listing date, and the "date listed" column contains the dates with years, we need to group by the year part of the "date listed" column to count the sites listed in 1980. We can use the f_group_column(date listed) operation to group the sites based on their listing year to analyze this situation.

The dates listed in the table are March 11, 1982; May 31, 1980; November 13, 1980; December 8, 1980; November 13, 1980; February 29, 1980; May 23, 1980; and November 28, 1972. Among these, the dates that fall in the year 1980 are May 31, 1980; November 13, 1980; December 8, 1980; November 13, 1980; February 29, 1980; and May 23, 1980. Therefore, there were 6 sites listed in 1980.
